root_text.sn35.158:1.1: “Cakkhuṁ, bhikkhave, yoniso manasi karotha, cakkhāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassatha.
root_text.sn35.158:1.2: Cakkhuṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yoniso manasikaronto, cakkhāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassanto cakkhusmimpi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.158:1.8: jivhaṁ, bhikkhave, yoniso manasi karotha, jivhāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassatha.
root_text.sn35.158:1.9: Jivhaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yoniso manasikaronto, jivhāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassanto jivhāyapi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.158:1.14: manaṁ, bhikkhave, yoniso manasi karotha, manāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassatha.
root_text.sn35.158:1.15: Manaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yoniso manasikaronto, manāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassanto manasmimpi nibbindati.

translation_text.sn35.158:0.3: Focus, the Interior, and the End of Relishing
translation_text.sn35.158:1.1: “Mendicants, rationally apply the mind to the eye. Truly see the impermanence of the eye.
translation_text.sn35.158:1.2: When a mendicant does this, they grow disillusioned with the eye.
translation_text.sn35.158:1.3: When relishing ends, greed ends. When greed ends, relishing ends.
translation_text.sn35.158:1.5: When relishing and greed end, the mind is said to be well freed.
translation_text.sn35.158:1.6: Rationally apply the mind to the ear …
translation_text.sn35.158:1.14: mind. Truly see the impermanence of the mind.
translation_text.sn35.158:1.15: When a mendicant does this, they grow disillusioned with the mind.
translation_text.sn35.158:1.16: When relishing ends, greed ends. When greed ends, relishing ends.
translation_text.sn35.158:1.18: When relishing and greed end, the mind is said to be well freed.”
